ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange w3wp.exe dropping .aspx web shell into OAB/ECP/OWA virtual directory
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM post-exploitation step from the ProxyLogon campaign and its 2024-2025 successors: an IIS worker process (w3wp.exe / UMWorkerProcess.exe) writing an .aspx/.ashx/.asmx file under an on-prem Exchange virtual directory. This is the file-write side of CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 and the exact tradecraft Microsoft and CrowdStrike (MURKY PANDA) attribute to this cluster's edge-device-to-on-prem pivot.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="UMWorkerProcess.exe") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\V15\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\V14\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") by host Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\",
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\",
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\"
]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any (_exchange_paths)
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !in~ ("system","network service","local service") or true   // keep system context — w3wp runs as IIS AppPool by default
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, FileSize,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\",
    @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\",
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\"
]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
| where TargetFilePath has_any (_exchange_paths)
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
          ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) - Exchange Web Shell Drop in Virtual Directory
id: 7e3f1c9a-3c2c-4f1d-9d8b-9b0a4f2e6e21
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects an IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) or Unified Messaging worker (UMWorkerProcess.exe)
  creating .aspx/.ashx/.asmx files in on-prem Exchange virtual directories - the canonical
  ProxyLogon-era and post-2021 Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM / MURKY PANDA) post-exploitation artifact.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers-with-0-day-exploits/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
      - '\ClientAccess\'
      - '\aspnet_client\'
      - '\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V15\'
      - '\Microsoft\Exchange Server\V14\'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange CU/SU installation activity
  - Authorised OWA customisation deployment
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (Silk Typhoon / formerly HAFNIUM, MITRE G0125) and CrowdStrike (MURKY PANDA) both anchor the actor's signature on Exchange 0-day chaining (ProxyLogon CVE-2021-26855 etc.) culminating in an .aspx web shell dropped by the SYSTEM-context w3wp.exe IIS worker - documented filenames include supp0rt.aspx, HttpProxy.aspx, aspnet_iisstart.aspx, and 8-char random GUIDs. Generic web-shell rules loo

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — LSASS MiniDump via comsvcs.dll / ProcDump from Exchange or web-shell context
ucs.description: Detects the credential-access stage Silk Typhoon ran on every confirmed Exchange ProxyLogon victim: rundll32.exe invoking comsvcs.dll's MiniDump export against lsass.exe (or ProcDump64.exe -ma lsass) - frequently spawned as a child of w3wp.exe via the dropped web shell. This is the credential-theft pair to UC1 and is what enabled lateral movement and 365-mailbox exfiltration in the 2021 and 2025 campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*minidump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*")) OR ((Processes.process_name="procdump.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump64.exe") (Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="* -ma *")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"   // keep SYSTEM-context invocations from IIS worker
| where
    (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","minidump","#24"))
    or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
    or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
        and FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs")
    or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountSid,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$" or ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| where
    (NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
        and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "minidump" or CommandLine has "#24"))
    or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe")
        and CommandLine has "lsass")
    or (ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
        and NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
        and CommandLine has "comsvcs")
    or (NewProcessName endswith_cs "\\powershell.exe"
        and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName, SubjectUserSid
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) - LSASS Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump or ProcDump
id: 2b8f4a51-5a7f-4d9a-8c4b-1e8c2d3b9a47
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects LSASS credential dumping using rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump (export #24)
  or ProcDump -ma against lsass.exe. Pairs with Silk Typhoon's Exchange web shell
  drop (UC1) - the actor characteristically launches this from a w3wp.exe-spawned cmd
  or PowerShell in a single intrusion chain.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers-with-0-day-exploits/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/adversaries/murky-panda/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.011
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_comsvcs:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - 'minidump'
      - '#24'
  selection_procdump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  selection_iis_chain:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs'
  condition: 1 of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Authorised red-team / purple-team exercises
  - Sysinternals troubleshooting under change control
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon DFIR write-ups and the original 2021 Volexity disclosure both call out rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> lsass.dmp full and procdump64.exe -accepteula -ma lsass as the actor's two LSASS-extraction primitives, often launched as children of w3wp.exe via the dropped web shell. The IIS-parented variant (selection_iis_chain) is essentially exc
